text.heading: Maritime pollution: NIMASA D-G, four others honoured by OAU
text.p: THE Nigerian Maritime Administration and Safety Agency Director-General, Dr Shamsideen Dosunmu, has been honoured by the Obafemi Awolowo University for his contributions to marine pollution control and sustainable development of the elosystem.

Also honoured by the university are Mr Donald Duke, former governor of Cross River State, Chief Philip Asiodu, president of the Nigerian Conservation Foundation and former Presidential Adviser on Economic Matters, Prof Ajayi Borofice, Director-General, National Space Development and Research Agency and Dr Ime Ikopido, former Minister in charge of Environment.
In a statement, Hajia Lani Tumaka, Head , Public Relations , NIMASA, said the award was given at the silver jubilee celebration of the university’s Institute of Ecology and Environment Studies.

Presenting the award, the Vice- Chancellor, Prof Michael Faborode, said the university community was happy to see one of the alumni being honoured. He acknowledged NIMASA’s support to the institute and expressed the hope that the existing working relationship between the two institutions would be strengthened.
Faborode enjoined NIMASA and other recipients to see the recognition as a tonic to do more for the cause of a clean and sustainable development of the environment.
Vice President Dr Goodluck Jonathan, in his goodwill message, congratulated the institute on its silver jubilee and ground- breaking achievement.

The Vice Ppresident urged the institute to be at the forefront of environmental teaching, research and publications and play a leading role to ensure that Nigeria meets the challenges of sustainable development.
He assured the institute that President Musa Yar ‘Adua’s administration was ready to complement with effort of the institute in Environmental Studies.

Reacting to the award, Dosunmu said: "I feel very honoured to be recognised by my alma mater. It is very rare for someone to pass through an institution out of thousands and be singled out among the multitude for honour. I feel so honoured. What we have been able to achieve so far is due to the unflinching support given to us by our ministry and the co-operation of stakeholders. I want to equally share this honour with members of my management team and the entire staff of the agency."
The award was preceded by a lecture on "The environment, tourism and good governance", by Mr Donald Duke.

The former governor aptly called Mr Tourism for his achievements in the development of the environment and tourism during his eight-year tenure, shared his experience in the drive to transform his state into a tourist paradise.

He spoke on the interface between environment and tourism and how commitment to sustainable development of the environment would impact positively on tourism, which is today regarded as the world’s biggest industry.
